headline: Geopolitical Shock Floods the Tape as Sellers Dominate Mid-Session
body_html:
Wednesday's session has turned decidedly defensive at midday, with declining issues outpacing advancers by nearly three to one across our 396-name universe — 292 stocks lower against just 100 in the green. The catalyst is unmistakable: President Trump's declaration that an interim accord with Iran is finished sent oil sharply higher and rattled equity sentiment broadly, with gold retreating more than one percent as crude-linked names absorbed the safe-haven rotation that might otherwise have buoyed precious metals. Homebuilders are the session's deepest wound, off more than three percent as a group, while Materials, Consumer, and Software each surrender more than one and a half percent.

Against that backdrop, Semiconductors and Energy stand as the session's lone bright spots, advancing 1.07% and 0.95%, respectively. Kirby Corporation leads all gainers with a 7.1% surge, joined by infrastructure contractor MasTec — up 5.86% — and offshore driller Valaris, which has added 4.61% on the rising geopolitical premium in crude markets. Akamai and connectivity-chip maker Credo Technology round out a thin but notable list of winners.

On the downside, building-products names Builders FirstSource and Carlisle Companies are both off nearly five percent, dragging Industrials into the red despite select bright spots. ResMed is the session's heaviest Healthcare casualty, shedding 4.9%. Into the close, traders will be watching Federal Reserve meeting minutes for any signal on the rate path and monitoring Middle East headline flow for further escalation around the Strait of Hormuz.

briefs.headline: Trump's Iran Declaration Jolts Risk Appetite at the Open
briefs.body: President Trump's announcement that a tentative peace framework with Iran has collapsed sent shockwaves through equity markets at the opening bell Wednesday. Oil prices jumped on the news while stocks retreated broadly, with the geopolitical shift overshadowing most domestic economic data. The tone remains fragile as investors await further diplomatic developments.

briefs.headline: Hormuz Risk Premium Returns as Damaged LNG Tanker Awaits Recovery
briefs.body: A Qatari liquefied-natural-gas tanker struck in recent hostilities is awaiting salvage operations, according to reports, stoking fresh concern about supply disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz. Energy-services stocks have responded positively mid-session, with offshore driller Valaris gaining 4.61% as traders price in elevated operational risk in the region.

briefs.headline: NATO Summit Fractures Surface Around Spain and Greenland
briefs.body: Tensions at the ongoing NATO summit widened Wednesday as President Trump ordered a halt to U.S. trade with Spain over disagreements on defence spending and Iran policy, while separately pressing demands regarding Greenland. The developments injected additional uncertainty into transatlantic markets and complicated allied unity messaging at the gathering.

briefs.headline: Fed Minutes Loom as Rate-Path Debate Sharpens
briefs.body: This afternoon's release of Federal Reserve meeting minutes is drawing unusual attention, with analysts expecting the record to reflect significant internal disagreement over the trajectory of interest rates. The divergence among policymakers is seen as potentially prolonging rate uncertainty well into the second half of 2026, a headwind for rate-sensitive sectors already under pressure today.

briefs.headline: Kirby Corporation Surges to Lead the Tape on Heavy Volume
briefs.body: Marine-transport operator Kirby Corporation has vaulted 7.1% to claim the session's top spot among gainers, a standout move in an otherwise broadly negative tape. The advance is amplified by thematic interest in domestic waterway logistics as geopolitical friction raises questions about international shipping routes, though company-specific catalysts have not been confirmed at midday.

briefs.headline: Homebuilders Bear the Brunt as Sector Drops More Than Three Percent
briefs.body: The Homebuilders group is Wednesday's worst-performing sector, declining 3.17% at midday, with Builders FirstSource off 5.48% and Carlisle Companies down 4.85% leading the retreat. Persistent rate uncertainty ahead of the Fed minutes, combined with a broader risk-off posture, has weighed heavily on names most sensitive to financing conditions and housing-market momentum.
